plan.title: Per-invocation tool telemetry via single choke-point tap in wrap()
plan.consumer_purpose: Capture timing, success/error, and output metrics for every tool invocation through the universal wrap() function, writing structured JSONL records queryable by the existing analytics tool.

root_causes.hypothesis: All ~80 tools pass through a single wrap() function in tool/tool.ts (~line 144-253), making it the universal choke point for telemetry instrumentation
evidence: Tool.define() at tool.ts:255 calls wrap(id, resolved, truncate, agents) for every tool
Tool.init() at tool.ts:275 calls info.init() which returns the wrapped definition
Every tool in the registry uses Tool.define() — grep confirms no bypass
The execute closure inside wrap() constructs the full pipeline: decode → execute → truncate, then wraps with retry, timeout, semaphore, span

fixes.description: Add tap telemetry around the execution pipeline in wrap() — one Success record per tool call after all layers (retry/timeout/semaphore) but before Effect.orDie
risks: R1 (MEDIUM): First use of `Effect.tap` + `tapError` in codebase — needs careful .catchAll wrapping so telemetry I/O failures never propagate
R2 (MEDIUM): Buffer.byteLength requires Buffer import (available globally in Bun, but pyright may complain)

R3 (LOW): InstanceState.context fallback to cwd() during tool registry bootstrap could write telemetry to wrong directory — mitigated by telemetry only firing on actual tool execution (not define time)
R4 (LOW): Per-file appendQueue in filesystem.ts serializes JSONL writes — safe but could create head-of-line blocking under extreme concurrency
validation: 1. bun test packages/opencode/test/tool/tool-define.test.ts — all existing tests pass unchanged
2. Write unit test: execute a tool, read tool_invocations.v1.jsonl, verify record schema
3. Write unit test: execute a tool that throws ToolError, verify error_kind is 'ToolError'
4. Write unit test: corrupt filesystem, verify tool still succeeds despite telemetry failure
5. bun typecheck from packages/opencode — no new type errors
6. Run full tool test suite: bun test packages/opencode/test/tool/

fixes.description: Extend analytics tool to query tool_invocations.v1.jsonl
fixes.before:
Current analytics.ts processes: bash, smart, heartbeat, feedback metrics.
The summary object has: session_count, bash_calls, smart_calls, heartbeats, feedback
risks: R5 (LOW): analytics.txt is a plain text file — ensure new metric name is listed in the description
validation: 1. After fix-A is applied and tools execute, call analytics tool with metric=tool_invocations

fixes.description: Create integration test for tool telemetry
fixes.after:
New test file with the following test cases:

Test 1: 'writes success record on tool execution'
  - Define a minimal tool, execute it with makeCtx()
  - Verify tool_invocations.v1.jsonl exists and contains one JSON line
  - Verify record has: tool, session, agent, latency_ms > 0, success=true, output_bytes > 0

Test 2: 'writes error record with error_kind on ToolError'
  - Define a tool that throws ToolError
  - Execute it, capture the exit
  - Verify JSONL record has: success=false, error_kind='ToolError', error_detail present

Test 3: 'maps all 5 error kinds correctly'
  - Test InvalidArgumentsError, ToolError, TimeoutError, TransientError, ValidationError
  - Each should produce the correct error_kind string

Test 4: 'unknown errors map to UnknownError'
  - Throw a plain Error (not a tagged error)
  - Verify error_kind is 'UnknownError'

Test 5: 'telemetry failure does not break tool execution'
  - Use a mock filesystem that throws on appendLine
  - Verify the tool still returns its result successfully
  - Verify the tap effect was caught and swallowed

Test 6: 'analytics tool queries tool_invocations metric'
  - After fix-A and fix-B, call analytics tool with metric='tool_invocations'
  - Verify the summary includes non-zero tool_invocations count
risks: R6 (MEDIUM): Test 5 requires mocking AppFileSystem.Service — need to verify the mock pattern works with Effect layers. Use Layer.succeed to override the service.
R7 (LOW): Test isolation — different tests writing to the same session directory could read each other's records. Mitigation: use SessionID.descending() to create unique session IDs per test.

plan.rollback_plan: Revert each fix in reverse order: remove test file, revert analytics.ts changes, remove telemetry block from tool.ts and restore original pipeline. No schema migrations needed — JSONL is append-only and optional.
plan.scope_note: THIS PLAN COVERS BUILTIN TOOLS ONLY (~80 tools via wrap()). Foundation subagent confirmed 3 execution paths: (1) builtin via wrap(), (2) plugin via registry.ts:fromPlugin(), (3) MCP via session/tools.ts:139-200. Plugin and MCP tools bypass wrap() entirely. Instrumenting the true universal convergence at session/tools.ts:resolve() is a larger change — deferred to a future lane per 'smallest possible change' directive.

pre_existing_risks.description: After a single I/O failure, appendQueues Map permanently poisons all future writes to the same file. Affects ALL 26+ appendLine callers, not just telemetry. Fix: delete Map entry on rejection. Published as blocker finding.

error_taxonomy_mapping.InvalidArgumentsError: Schema decode failure — LLM called tool with wrong args. instanceof InvalidArgumentsError → 'InvalidArgumentsError'
error_taxonomy_mapping.ToolError: General tool execution failure. instanceof ToolError → 'ToolError'
error_taxonomy_mapping.TimeoutError: Tool exceeded its timeout Duration. instanceof TimeoutError → 'TimeoutError'
error_taxonomy_mapping.TransientError: Temporary failure, retry may help. instanceof TransientError → 'TransientError'
error_taxonomy_mapping.unknown: Any other error type (plain Error, Effect defects, etc.) → 'UnknownError'

plan.adaptation_note: Original design specified `Effect.tapBoth({ onSuccess, onFailure })` but Effect v4.0.0-beta.66 only has `Effect.tap` and `Effect.tapError`. The plan chains `Effect.tap` (success) + `Effect.tapError` (failure) to achieve equivalent functionality. Each tap callback is independently wrapped in `Effect.catchAll(() => Effect.void)` to prevent telemetry failures from propagating.
